project_description: Build a PDF chatbot for internal company documents

selected_tools.rationale: Clerk provides hosted authentication with prebuilt UI components, so you don't need to implement login flows yourself. Tradeoff: vendor dependency and cost considerations once you scale past the free tier.
Supabase gives you a managed PostgreSQL database with a generous free tier and simple client SDK. Tradeoff: less control over database configuration than a dedicated Postgres provider.

Pinecone is a managed vector database purpose-built for semantic search, requiring minimal infrastructure knowledge. Tradeoff: managed cost at scale; consider Qdrant self-hosted if you expect high query volume.
LlamaParse handles PDF extraction reliably including tables and figures, with a simple API. Tradeoff: per-page pricing model; for high document volumes consider Unstructured self-hosted.
Vercel AI SDK simplifies streaming responses and tool calling in Next.js with minimal boilerplate. Tradeoff: TypeScript/Node.js only; use LangChain if you need Python.
Anthropic's Claude models perform well on document understanding and citation tasks. Tradeoff: API cost per token; consider caching frequent queries.
Next.js handles both frontend and API routes in a single project, reducing complexity for prototypes. Tradeoff: more opinionated than a standalone backend + frontend setup.
Vercel's zero-config deployment integrates natively with Next.js and handles scaling automatically. Tradeoff: vendor lock-in with Next.js features; Railway or Fly.io offer more portability.

architecture_rationale: This stack minimizes infrastructure decisions for a TypeScript beginner building a prototype. Clerk + Supabase + Vercel form a managed trifecta that handles auth, data, and deployment without DevOps. Pinecone + LlamaParse + Vercel AI SDK create a coherent RAG pipeline that integrates naturally with Next.js. The main risk is managed service costs at scale — this architecture is optimized for getting to a working prototype quickly, not for cost efficiency at volume.
